step_by_step_reasoning: We identify the user actor as UserCompanyAgent representing independent insurance agents. The agents access a single page application via CloudFront. CloudFront pulls static web assets from S3. Dynamic API requests are routed by CloudFront to AWS API Gateway. API Gateway triggers a custom Lambda Authorizer for access control. Valid requests are sent to the Python Lambda backend, which handles modernized business logic. The backend Lambda caches user session details in DynamoDB and forwards unmigrated/legacy logic requests to the legacy Tomcat application running on EC2.

graph.name: American Family Insurance - Modernizing Legacy Applications with Serverless
graph.notes: Main Street America modernized a legacy Tomcat application using a strangler pattern, replacing frontend hosting with S3/CloudFront and API services with API Gateway and Lambda, while keeping some legacy code on EC2.

nodes.name: Independent Agents
nodes.notes: Agents accessing the application to sell policies.

nodes.name: CloudFront CDN
nodes.notes: Caches and serves the single-page application frontend.

nodes.name: Legacy Tomcat Application
nodes.notes: Runs legacy Java code for non-migrated functions.

edges.notes: Call legacy APIs on Tomcat server.
